title: Birth of Savarni Manu and Description of the Next Manvataras

analysis.core_story_summary: Maitreya says- "O sage, now kindly narrate about the Manvantaras that are to come in future. " Parashar says- Vishwakarma’s daughter Sangya was the wife of Surya. They had three children, Manu, Yama and Yamani.

audience_notes.children_8_12: Use simple language, explain divine names as characters in a sacred legend.
audience_notes.teens_13_17: Connect events to decision-making, identity, and consequences.
audience_notes.young_adults_18_30: Highlight ethics, leadership, and social relevance.
audience_notes.devotional_traditional: Preserve sacred framing and theological terms respectfully.
audience_notes.global_general: Keep cultural context brief and avoid insider assumptions.

sensitivity_notes: Includes intense mythic elements (curses, demons, destruction).
References family and marriage roles rooted in traditional contexts.

redesigned_story_by_demographic.children_8_12: **Birth of Savarni Manu and Description of the Next Manvataras — Kids (8-12):** Maitreya says- "O sage, now kindly narrate about the Manvantaras that are to come in future. " Parashar says- Vishwakarma’s daughter Sangya was the wife of Surya. They had three children, Manu, Yama and Yamani. This story teaches dedication and sacrifice, righteous duty, inner focus and self-mastery. Think of it like a legend that helps us choose kindness, courage, and respect.
